tool_input.summary: Emissivity anomalies at Idunn Mons and Maat Mons are commonly interpreted as relatively unweathered (fresh) volcanic materials; on Venus that typically implies geologically young lava flows (likely << the ~hundreds-of-Myr mean surface age, plausibly in the ~10^5–10^6+ year range, depending on assumed weathering rates). In the PDS catalog here, the most directly relevant emissivity products are Magellan radiometry-derived emissivity (SCVDR EDF; also appears in GVDR) plus supporting Magellan radiometry/topography and SAR mosaics for geologic context. No Venus Express/VIRTIS surface-emissivity dataset was found in the current catalog search results.

candidates.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: SCVDR includes Magellan thermal microwave radiometry–derived surface emissivity estimates; these are the key PDS products to reproduce/inspect emissivity patterns that can be used to infer relative freshness/age of volcanic surfaces.

candidates.title: Magellan SCVDR Emissivity Data File (EDF) Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: This is the specific SCVDR emissivity (EDF) collection; it’s the most direct dataset for mapping emissivity variations potentially linked to young, less-weathered lavas at edifices such as Idunn Mons and Maat Mons.

candidates.title: Magellan ARCDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: ARCDR contains composite altimetry and radiometry products; useful for tying emissivity anomalies to elevation/slope and for cross-checking radiometry-derived surface properties relevant to volcanic surface state/age interpretations.

candidates.title: Magellen ARCDR Radiometry Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Radiometry composites provide additional context for surface thermal/microwave properties that relate to emissivity and can support assessing whether anomalies reflect fresh volcanic materials.

candidates.title: Magellan Global Vector Data Record (GVDR) Bundle
candidates.reasoning: GVDR is a gridded summary of Magellan scattering/emission results (derived in part from SCVDR). Useful for regional-scale comparisons of emissivity-related parameters around major volcanoes.

candidates.title: Magellan GVDR Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: The GVDR data collection provides the actual gridded products for global/regional analysis, complementary to the orbit-by-orbit SCVDR EDF emissivity values.

candidates.title: Magellan MIDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: MIDR SAR mosaics provide the geomorphic/geologic context (flows, vents, roughness) needed to interpret whether emissivity anomalies correspond to young flow units versus other causes.

dataset.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: The Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) is an orbit-by-orbit reduction of Magellan scattering and emission measurements carried out at Stanford University. The SCVDR includes near-nadir scattering functions obtained by numerical inversion from altimetry (ALT) echoes, results (e.g., rms surface slopes and Fresnel reflectivity) from fitting analytic functions to those inversions, scattering function segments at oblique incidence angles derived from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) echoes, and estimates of surface emissivity derived from thermal microwave radiometry (RAD) measurements. The SCVDR is one of several inputs to the Global Vector Data Record (GVDR), a gridded summary of scattering results, also produced at Stanford.

dataset.title: Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: The Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) is an orbit-by-orbit reduction of Magellan scattering and emission measurements carried out at Stanford University. The SCVDR includes near-nadir scattering functions obtained by numerical inversion from altimetry (ALT) echoes, results (e.g., rms surface slopes and Fresnel reflectivity) from fitting analytic functions to those inversions, scattering function segments at oblique incidence angles derived from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) echoes, and estimates of surface emissivity derived from thermal microwave radiometry (RAD) measurements. The SCVDR is one of several inputs to the Global Vector Data Record (GVDR), a gridded summary of scattering results, also produced at Stanford.

dataset.title: Magellan Global Vector Data Record (GVDR) Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains composite radar scattering functions derived from Magellan synthetic aperture radar, altimetry, and radiometry. Results are stored in ASCII and binary tables linked to pixels with 18.225 km resolution. Pixels are organized in sinusoidal equal area, Mercator, north polar stereographic, and south polar stereographic projections.

parts.args: {"summary":"Emissivity anomalies at Idunn Mons and Maat Mons are commonly interpreted as relatively unweathered (fresh) volcanic materials; on Venus that typically implies geologically young lava flows (likely << the ~hundreds-of-Myr mean surface age, plausibly in the ~10^5–10^6+ year range, depending on assumed weathering rates). In the PDS catalog here, the most directly relevant emissivity products are Magellan radiometry-derived emissivity (SCVDR EDF; also appears in GVDR) plus supporting Magellan radiometry/topography and SAR mosaics for geologic context. No Venus Express/VIRTIS surface-emissivity dataset was found in the current catalog search results.", "candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:magellan_scvdr::1.0","path":null,"title":"Magellan Surface Characteristics Vector Data Record (SCVDR) Bundle","mission":"Magellan","node":"geo","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"SCVDR includes Magellan thermal microwave radiometry–derived surface emissivity estimates;
